Hero.text: Donnel Arryn serves as Lord of the Eyrie and head of House Arryn. He rules the Vale of Arryn from the Eyrie and holds authority over the Vale’s bannermen. In his youth, Donnel was a ward of House Targaryen and grew up alongside King Daeron II Targaryen. Recently wed, he received a set of imported armor from the Crownlands, crafted and improved by the master smiths on the Street of Steel and financed by King Daeron as a wedding gift. His younger sister Alys also married Daeron’s youngest son, Prince Rhaegel Targaryen. Donnel is a staunch House Targaryn supporter and a loyalist.

{=QyFdfZrC}Jeyne Arryn became Lady of the Eyrie in 97 AC at the age of three after her father and brothers were killed by the Stone Crows. During her minority, Yorbert Royce served as regent. Her cousin Arnold Arryn twice contested her rule and was imprisoned in a sky cell; he and his descendants were later disinherited in her final testament. During the Dance of the Dragons, she supported Rhaenyra Targaryen after treating with Jacaerys Velaryon, bringing the Vale into the war on the side of the blacks. She later served as one of the regents for Aegon III Targaryen. Jeyne died in 134 AC at age forty in Gulltown and named Joffrey Arryn as her heir in her will.

Laena Velaryon was a noblewoman of House Velaryon, the daughter of Lord Alyn Velaryon and his wife, Lady Baela Targaryen, and the half-niece of King Aegon III Targaryen. She was named after her paternal great-aunt and maternal grandmother, Lady Laena Velaryon.

Baela Targaryen was a princess of House Targaryen, the elder daughter of Prince Daemon Targaryen and Lady Laena Velaryon. She and her twin sister Rhaena were born during the reign of King Viserys I Targaryen. Baela became a dragonrider and was bonded to the dragon Moondancer. She was raised among the Targaryen court and her father’s household during the growing succession crisis that later led to the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Baela supported Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen and the Black faction. She fought in the Battle Above the Gods Eye against King Aegon II Targaryen, where Moondancer was killed and Baela was severely injured before being captured. After the war, she was released and later married Alyn Velaryon, who became Lord of the Tides. She had children with him and lived during the reign of King Aegon III Targaryen.
